_doc: Excel → Notion 'Content Calendar' migration mapping for T-044. Loaded by scripts/migrate-content-calendar.js. See docs/content-calendar-schema.md for the Notion property contract. See PRISM-Vault/Admin/CoS/decisions-log.md (2026-05-21) for the multi-select Platform + Linked Asset split resolutions, and the non-data-row skip rules.

sheet_filter._doc: A sheet is processed only if its first 10 rows contain a row matching this header sequence (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed). Sheets that don't match (Overview / KPIs / Tooling & Budget / Pod Roles) are skipped with a 'no-header' note in the report.

non_data_row_rules._doc: Per resolved 2026-05-21 — T-044 skips rows where column A spans all 8 columns (section header), or where columns B–H are all NULL.

column_to_property._doc: Maps Excel column header → Notion property. Unmapped values fall to the named default and surface in the report's 'Unmapped values' section so Michele can spot-check.

Day._note: Excel column is labeled 'Day' but the contents are phase tags ('Foundation', 'Pre-Launch', 'Launch Week', etc.) — closer to a Series tag than a weekday.

platform_map._doc: Split Channel cell on '/' and '+', strip parentheticals (everything from '(' onward), trim, then look up. Format-as-platform aliases (Shorts → YouTube, Reels → Instagram, LinkedIn Live → LinkedIn) are intentional: in the Excel calendar these are written as channels but they're really platform-bound formats. Unmapped values default to N/A and surface in the report.

type_map._doc: Format cell → Notion Type. Unmapped → 'Other' (logged in report).

status_map._doc: Excel Status → Notion Status. 'Planned' is the Excel default; T-044 maps it to Notion 'Draft' (committed to a date but not yet published).
